error.signature: AI gives universal self-defense legal advice without jurisdiction-specific warnings

verdict.summary: Self-defense laws vary enormously by jurisdiction. Stand Your Ground (US: some states), Duty to Retreat (US: other states, most of Europe, Japan), Castle Doctrine (varies). Advice legal in Texas may be murder in Japan.

dead_ends.action: Give universal self-defense legal advice without specifying jurisdiction
dead_ends.why_fails: Stand Your Ground (Florida, Texas): no duty to retreat anywhere. Duty to Retreat (most of Europe, Japan, some US states): must flee if safely possible. UK: 'reasonable force' (subjective). Japan: extremely strict proportional force requirement. Wrong advice = prison.

Castle Doctrine (right to use force in your home) varies dramatically. US (most states): broad protection. UK: 'disproportionate force' test. Germany: must attempt retreat even at home in some cases. Some countries require you to flee your own home.

workarounds.action: ALWAYS state: 'Self-defense laws vary significantly by jurisdiction. This is general information, not legal advice. Consult a local attorney for your specific situation.' Then provide jurisdiction-specific information if the user's location is known.

Key distinctions to communicate: Stand Your Ground vs. Duty to Retreat, proportional force requirements, what constitutes 'reasonable' force, castle doctrine scope, and when deadly force is legally justified (varies by state/country).
